_source: editor/data (read-only); move descriptions + egg moves intentionally omitted

1.name: Stench
1.desc: By releasing a stench when the Pokémon deals damage with its moves, there is a 10% chance that targets will flinch. This does not stack with moves or items that have a chance to flinch.

2.name: Drizzle
2.desc: Drizzle summons rain weather when the Pokemon enters battle, lasting 8 turns (12 with Damp Rock held). Rain boosts Water moves by 50% and cuts Fire damage by 50%. Certain moves and abilities have special interactions with rain. Cannot override primal weather conditions.

3.name: Speed Boost
3.desc: Boosts the Pokémon’s Speed stat at the end of every turn.

4.name: Battle Armor
4.desc: Incoming damage is reduced by 20% (x0.8), multiplicative with other damage reduction. Additionally, critical hits are blocked, functioning as regular hits and not activating on-crit effects like To The Bone's bleed.

5.name: Sturdy
5.desc: If the Pokémon has full HP and takes damage from a move that would knock it out in one hit, it will endure the hit with 1 HP. The Pokémon is also immune to one-hit KO moves.

6.name: Damp
6.desc: If the user makes contact with another Pokemon, offensively or defensively, the other Pokemon's type is changed to pure Water. This happens immediately for multihit or priority moves and can remove STAB in the middle of an enemy's multihit move.

7.name: Limber
7.desc: All recoil damage to the user is halved; this includes moves with scaling recoil damage like Brave Bird, moves with crash damage like Jump Kick, or moves with fixed recoil like Steel Beam. Paralysis also cannot affect the user, and if they gain Limber, existing paralysis is cured.

8.name: Sand Veil
8.desc: Boosts the Pokémon’s evasiveness by 25% in a sandstorm. The Pokémon takes no damage from sandstorms if it would otherwise.

9.name: Static
9.desc: 30% chance to paralyze on contact, 10% on non-contact.

10.name: Volt Absorb
10.desc: Electric-type moves do not work on the Pokémon. Instead, they restore ¼ of its max HP.

11.name: Water Absorb
11.desc: Water-type moves do not work on the Pokémon. Instead, they restore ¼ of its max HP.

12.name: Oblivious
12.desc: This Pokemon is immune to infatuation, Scare, Intimidate and Taunt. When gaining this ability while afflicted by any of the mentioned statuses, instantly cures them.

14.name: Compound Eyes
14.desc: The Pokémon’s compound eyes boost the accuracy of its moves by 30%.

15.name: Insomnia
15.desc: The Pokémon’s insomnia prevents it from becoming drowsy or being put to sleep.

16.name: Color Change
16.desc: Transforms the user into the best defensive type before taking damage. Prioritizes immunities over resistances. Only changes to a pure type. Once per turn.

17.name: Immunity
17.desc: Prevents poisoning. Reduces all Poison-type damage by 50%. Multiplicative with other sources of damage reduction. If poisoned when gaining this ability, the poison is immediately cured.

18.name: Flash Fire
18.desc: If hit by a Fire-type move, the Pokémon absorbs the flames and uses them to power up its own Fire-type moves by 50%.

19.name: Shield Dust
19.desc: Protective dust shields the Pokémon, making it immune to additional effects from attacks.

20.name: Own Tempo
20.desc: Grants immunity to confusion, Intimidate, and Scare. Immediately cures confusion when receiving this ability.

21.name: Suction Cups
21.desc: The Pokémon uses suction cups to stay in one spot. This protects it from moves and items that would force it to switch out.

22.name: Intimidate
22.desc: When the Pokémon enters a battle, it intimidates opposing Pokémon and makes them cower, lowering their Attack stats.

23.name: Shadow Tag
23.desc: Prevents all non-Shadow Tag or Ghost-type foes from switching out. Pokemon holding Shed Shell or using a pivot move such as Flip Turn can escape. Activates during the next turn if the user switches in mid battle.

24.name: Rough Skin
24.desc: When the Pokémon is hit by a contact move, its rough skin damages the attacker equal to ⅛ of their max HP.

25.name: Wonder Guard
25.desc: Its mysterious power only lets supereffective moves hit the Pokémon.

26.name: Levitate
26.desc: The user is immune to Ground- type moves and other ground effects such as Spikes and terrains. Boosts the damage of Flying-type moves by 25%.

27.name: Effect Spore
27.desc: When the Pokémon is hit by a contact move, the attacker has a 30% chance of being poisoned, paralyzed, or put to sleep.

28.name: Synchronize
28.desc: If the Pokémon is burned, paralyzed, poisoned, or badly poisoned by another Pokémon’s move or Ability, that Pokémon will also be inflicted with the same status condition. If this Pokémon is your lead into battle, opposing wild Pokémon will share its nature.

29.name: Clear Body
29.desc: The Pokémon’s stats cannot be lowered by other Pokémon’s moves or Abilities.

30.name: Natural Cure
30.desc: The Pokémon’s status conditions are cured when it switches out of battle.

31.name: Lightning Rod
31.desc: The user draws in Electric-type moves and gains immunity to them. Additionally, Electric-type moves boost the highest attacking stat of the user by one stage.

32.name: Serene Grace
32.desc: Doubles the likelihood of additional effects occurring when the Pokémon uses its moves. This does not affect items.

34.name: Chlorophyll
34.desc: Boosts the Pokemon's Speed by 50% during sun.

35.name: Illuminate
35.desc: Boosts the user's accuracy by 1.2x. Removes Ghost-typing on target when landing an attack.

36.name: Trace
36.desc: When the Pokémon enters a battle, it changes its Ability to match that of an opponent.

37.name: Huge Power
37.desc: Doubles the power of the Pokémon’s physical moves.

38.name: Poison Point
38.desc: Has a 30% chance to inflict poison on contact moves, both when attacking and being attacked.

39.name: Inner Focus
39.desc: Focus Blast never misses. Unaffected by flinch, Intimidate, or Scare.

40.name: Magma Armor
40.desc: Immune to frostbite. Reduces damage received from Water and Ice-type moves by 30%. Multiplicative with other sources of damage reduction.

41.name: Water Veil
41.desc: Uses Aqua Ring on entry, which restores 1/16 HP each turn. Grants immunity to burn status and removes burn on switching in.

42.name: Magnet Pull
42.desc: Prevents Steel-type Pokemon from switching out while the user is present. Ghost-types are immune to this effect. Pokemon holding Shed Shell or using a pivot move such as Flip Turn can escape. Activates during the next turn if the user switches in mid battle.

43.name: Soundproof
43.desc: Soundproofing gives the Pokémon full immunity to all sound-based moves.

44.name: Rain Dish
44.desc: Restores 1/8 max HP at the end of each turn in rain.

45.name: Sand Stream
45.desc: Summons a Sandstorm for 8 turns (12 with Smooth Rock) on entry. Damages non-Rock/Ground/Steel types by 1/16 HP per turn. Rock- types gain a 50% Special Defense boost. Halves the effectiveness of sun related moves.

46.name: Pressure
46.desc: Doubles PP usage of opposing moves and clears all positive stat stages on entry.

47.name: Thick Fat
47.desc: The Pokémon is protected by a layer of thick fat, which halves the damage the Pokémon takes from Fire- and Ice-type moves.

48.name: Early Bird
48.desc: The Pokémon awakens from sleep twice as fast as other Pokémon.

49.name: Flame Body
49.desc: Contact with this Pokemon has a 30% chance to inflict burn. Non- contact has a 20% chance. Works offensively and defensively.

50.name: Run Away
50.desc: Guarantees successful escape from wild Pokemon battles regardless of speed differences. When any of the user's stats are lowered by an opponent's move or ability, raises the user's Speed by 2 stages.

51.name: Keen Eye
51.desc: Prevents accuracy stat stage drops. All moves gain a 1.2x accuracy boost.

52.name: Hyper Cutter
52.desc: Hyper Cutter prevents enemies from lowering the user's Attack stat through moves or abilities. All contact moves used by this Pokemon have their critical hit rate increased by one stage.

53.name: Pickup
53.desc: Clears all entry hazards from your side of the field when entering battle. The Pokemon is still susceptible to hazards while clearing them.

54.name: Truant
54.desc: Each time the Pokémon uses a move, it spends the next turn loafing around.

55.name: Hustle
55.desc: Boosts the power of attacks by 1.4x but reduces their accuracy by 10%. Only affects non-status moves.

56.name: Cute Charm
56.desc: When hit by making contact (offensively or defensively), has a 50% chance to infatuate the attacker (cuts their Attack and Special Attack in half). This only works on Pokemon of the opposite gender.

57.name: Plus
57.desc: Doubles the Pokemon's offensive power when partnered with an ally that has Plus or Minus.

58.name: Minus
58.desc: Doubles the Pokemon's offensive power when partnered with an ally that has Minus or Plus.

59.name: Forecast
59.desc: The Pokémon transforms with the weather to change its type to Water, Fire, or Ice.

60.name: Sticky Hold
60.desc: The Pokémon’s held items cling to its sticky body and cannot be stolen or removed by other Pokémon.

61.name: Shed Skin
61.desc: The Pokémon has a 30% chance to cure its own status conditions at the end of every turn by shedding its skin.

62.name: Guts
62.desc: The Pokémon is so gutsy that when it has a status condition, its Attack stat is boosted by 50%. Being burned does not halve the damage dealt by the Pokémon’s physical moves.

63.name: Marvel Scale
63.desc: When the Pokémon has a status condition, its marvelous scales boost its Defense stat by 50%.

64.name: Liquid Ooze
64.desc: The strong stench of the Pokémon’s oozed liquid damages attackers that use HP-draining moves instead of healing them.

65.name: Overgrow
65.desc: Boosts the power of Grass-type moves by 20%, or by 50% at 1/3 HP or lower.

66.name: Blaze
66.desc: Boosts the power of Fire-type moves by 20%, or by 50% at 1/3 HP or lower.

67.name: Torrent
67.desc: Boosts the power of Water-type moves by 20%, or by 50% at 1/3 HP or lower.

68.name: Swarm
68.desc: Boosts the power of Bug-type moves by 20%, or by 50% at 1/3 HP or lower.

69.name: Rock Head
69.desc: Prevents all recoil damage from the user's moves and abilities. Also grants immunity to enrage recoil damage. Does not prevent crash damage or Explosion/Self- Destruct damage.

70.name: Drought
70.desc: Summons harsh sunlight for 8 turns (12 with Heat Rock) on entry. Boosts Fire moves by 50%, reduces Water moves by 50%. Activates extra effects on sun related moves.

71.name: Arena Trap
71.desc: Prevents all non-levitating or Ghost-type foes from switching out. Pokemon holding Shed Shell or using a pivot move such as Flip Turn can escape. Activates during the next turn if the user switches in mid battle

72.name: Vital Spirit
72.desc: Immune to sleep. When the Pokemon uses a Fighting-type move, it heals all status conditions immediately after the move resolves. Removes sleep when gained.

73.name: White Smoke
73.desc: Sets Smokescreen on entry, lasting 3 turns. Smokescreen increases the evasiveness of your party by 25%.

74.name: Pure Power
74.desc: Increases the user's Attack stat by 2x. Multiplicative with other damage boosts.

75.name: Shell Armor
75.desc: Incoming damage is reduced by 20% (x0.8), multiplicative with other damage reduction. Additionally, critical hits are blocked, functioning as regular hits and not activating on-crit effects like To The Bone's bleed.

76.name: Air Lock
76.desc: Sets Tailwind for 3 turns and clears all weather effects on entry. Primal weathers are suppressed. Weather can still be set while the user is on the field, but will have no effect until the user switches.

77.name: Tangled Feet
77.desc: When the user is confused or enraged, the Pokemon uses its Speed stat instead of Defense or Special Defense for damage calculations. Choice Scarf does not affect this ability.

78.name: Motor Drive
78.desc: The Pokémon takes no damage when hit by Electric-type moves. Instead, its Speed stat is boosted.

79.name: Rivalry
79.desc: The Pokémon’s competitive spirit boosts the power of its moves by 25% against targets of the same gender, and lowers it by 25% against targets of the opposite gender. The power remains the same as usual if either Pokémon’s gender is unknown.

80.name: Steadfast
80.desc: The Pokémon’s determination boosts its Speed stat when it flinches.

81.name: Snow Cloak
81.desc: Boosts the Pokémon’s evasiveness by 25% in snow or hail. The Pokémon takes no damage from hail if it would otherwise.

82.name: Gluttony
82.desc: Berries that normally activate at 1/4 HP trigger at 1/2 HP instead. Additionally, after consuming any berry, the user recovers 1/3 of max HP. Does not include berries consumed through moves like Pluck.

83.name: Anger Point
83.desc: When hit, raises the user's Attack by 1 stage or maximizes it on critical hits. Activates on each hit of a multihit move.

84.name: Unburden
84.desc: Doubles the Pokémon’s Speed stat when its held item is consumed or lost.

85.name: Heatproof
85.desc: The Pokémon’s heatproof body halves the damage taken from burns and Fire-type moves.

86.name: Simple
86.desc: Doubles the effects of the Pokémon’s stat changes.

87.name: Dry Skin
87.desc: Restores ¼ of the Pokémon’s max HP when hit by Water-type moves or ⅛ of the its max HP at the end of every turn in rain. However, the Pokémon receives 25% more damage from Fire-type moves and loses ⅛ of its max HP at the end of every turn in harsh sunlight.

88.name: Download
88.desc: The Pokémon compares an opposing Pokémon’s Defense and Sp. Def stats before raising its own Attack or Sp. Atk stat — whichever will be more effective.

89.name: Iron Fist
89.desc: Boosts the power of punching moves by 30%.

90.name: Poison Heal
90.desc: If poisoned or badly poisoned, the Pokémon has ⅛ of its max HP restored at the end of every turn instead of losing HP.

91.name: Adaptability
91.desc: Boosts the power of moves of the same type as the Pokémon by 100% instead of 50%. Does not stack with the Stellar-type Tera boost.

92.name: Skill Link
92.desc: The Pokémon’s multistrike moves always hit the max amount of times.

93.name: Hydration
93.desc: Cures the Pokémon’s status conditions at the end of every turn in rain.

94.name: Solar Power
94.desc: Boosts the Pokemon's highest attacking stat by 50% during sun.

95.name: Quick Feet
95.desc: When the Pokémon has a status condition, its Speed stat is boosted by 50%. Being paralyzed does not lower the Pokémon’s Speed stat.

96.name: Normalize
96.desc: Converts all damaging moves to Normal-type and grants a 10% power boost. Normal-type moves bypass resistances, but not immunities.

97.name: Sniper
97.desc: Boosts the power of the Pokémon’s critical hits by 125% instead of 50%.

98.name: Magic Guard
98.desc: The Pokémon takes damage only from attacks.

99.name: No Guard
99.desc: The Pokémon employs no-guard tactics to ensure incoming and outgoing attacks always land. If this Pokémon is your lead into battle, it raises the likelihood of double battles.

100.name: Stall
100.desc: Reduces damage by 30% if the user has not moved yet. Multiplicative with other damage reduction sources. Works when the user switches in mid-turn.

101.name: Technician
101.desc: Boosts the power of the Pokémon’s moves by 50% if their power is 60 or less.

102.name: Leaf Guard
102.desc: During sun, cures all status conditions at the end of the turn.

103.name: Klutz (N)
103.desc: Items do not work when held by the Pokémon.

104.name: Mold Breaker
104.desc: The Pokémon’s moves are unaffected by the Ability of the target, with certain exceptions.

105.name: Super Luck
105.desc: The Pokémon is so lucky that the critical-hit ratios of its moves are boosted by one stage.

106.name: Aftermath
106.desc: Uses a 100 BP Explosion or Outburst (whichever is higher) when knocked out. Using explosion moves will always Flinch the target.

107.name: Anticipation
107.desc: Alerts the Pokemon when facing opponents with super-effective moves on switch-in. Dodge the first super effective hit received in battle.

108.name: Forewarn
108.desc: Casts an 80 BP Future Sight on the opposing Pokemon when switching in. Strikes 2 turns later, bypassing substitutes and other protections. The attack cannot miss once initiated and ignores accuracy checks. This cannot target the same Pokemon twice.

109.name: Unaware
109.desc: The Pokémon ignores the target’s stat changes when attacking, and ignores the attacker’s stat changes when being attacked. It cannot ignore changes to the Speed stat, however.

110.name: Tinted Lens
110.desc: The Pokémon’s "not very effective" attacks deal double damage.

111.name: Filter
111.desc: Reduces damage from super- effective attacks by 35%. Multiplicative with other sources of damage reduction.

112.name: Slow Start
112.desc: Halves Attack, Special Attack, and Speed for the first 5 turns after switching in. The turn counter resets each time the Pokemon switches out.

113.name: Scrappy
113.desc: The user can land Normal and Fighting-type moves on Ghost- types for neutral damage. Grants immunity to Intimidate and Scare.

114.name: Storm Drain
114.desc: The user draws in Water-type moves and gains immunity to them. Additionally, Water-type moves boost the highest attacking stat of the user by one stage.

115.name: Ice Body
115.desc: Restores 1/8 max HP at the end of each turn in hail. Also grants immunity to hail damage.

116.name: Solid Rock
116.desc: Reduces damage from super- effective attacks by 35%. Multiplicative with other sources of damage reduction.

117.name: Snow Warning
117.desc: Summons hailstorm for 8 turns (12 with Icy Rock) on entry. Damages non-Ice types by 1/16 HP per turn. Boosts the Defense stat of Ice-type Pokemon by 50%. Halves the effectiveness of sun related moves.

118.name: Honey Gather
118.desc: The Pokémon gathers Honey which is then sold for money after a battle is won, even if it did not participate.

119.name: Frisk
119.desc: Upon entering battle, reveals the opponents' items and prevents them from working for 2 turns. Does not prevent Mega Stones and other similar items from working.

120.name: Reckless
120.desc: Increases the damage of moves that cause recoil by 20%. This bonus also applies when moves gain recoil through other means, such as madness or abilities.

121.name: Multitype
121.desc: Changes the Pokémon’s type to match the Plate it holds.

122.name: Flower Gift
122.desc: During sun, boost base Special Attack and Special Defense by 50% for this Pokemon and all allies.

123.name: Bad Dreams
123.desc: All sleeping opponents lose 25% of their max HP at the end of each turn.

124.name: Pickpocket
124.desc: When the Pokémon is hit by a contact move, it will steal a held item from the attacker.

125.name: Sheer Force
125.desc: Removes most beneficial secondary effects after landing attacks in exchange for a 1.3x boost. Notably prevents Life Orb recoil when using these moves. Removable effects include reducing the target's stats, increasing the user's stats, inflicting status on a target and flinching.

126.name: Contrary
126.desc: Reverses any stat changes affecting the Pokémon so that attempts to boost its stats instead lower them — and attempts to lower its stats will boost them.

127.name: Unnerve
127.desc: Unnerves opposing Pokémon and makes them unable to eat Berries.

128.name: Defiant
128.desc: When the Pokémon has any of its stats lowered by an opponent, its Attack stat is boosted sharply.

129.name: Defeatist
129.desc: Halves both Attack and Special Attack stats when HP drops below 33% of maximum. Deactivates after healing above this threshold.

130.name: Cursed Body
130.desc: When the Pokémon takes damage from a move, the attacker has a 30% chance to have whichever move it last used disabled for 4 turns.

131.name: Healer
131.desc: Gives a 30% chance to cure status conditions at the end of each turn for both the user and their ally if they are out in a double battle. Makes 2 separate checks for each Pokemon.

132.name: Friend Guard
132.desc: In a double battle, the user's ally receives 50% less damage. Multiplicative with other damage reduction sources.

133.name: Weak Armor
133.desc: When hit by a contact move, raises the user's Speed by 2 stages and lowers their defense by 1 stage. Activates on each hit of a multihit move.

134.name: Heavy Metal
134.desc: Takes half damage from Ghost and Dark-type attacks. Doubles the Pokemon's weight, which affects some moves.

135.name: Light Metal
135.desc: Light Metal halves the Pokemon's weight and boosts their Speed stat by 30%. Makes weight-based attacks such as Low Kick deal less damage against this Pokemon. However, it also decreases damage from the user's Heavy Slam and similar weight- based moves.

136.name: Multiscale
136.desc: Halves the damage the Pokémon takes while its HP is full.

137.name: Toxic Boost
137.desc: Increases the Pokemon's Attack stat by 50% when poisoned (regular or badly poisoned). Immediately applies poison to user when in Toxic Terrain, regardless of them being grounded or not. Nullifies poison damage.

138.name: Flare Boost
138.desc: When the Pokémon is burned, the power of its special moves are boosted by 50%.

139.name: Harvest
139.desc: If the Pokémon has used a Berry, it has a 50% chance of creating another one at the end of every turn. In harsh sunlight, the Pokémon is guaranteed to create a Berry.

140.name: Telepathy
140.desc: The Pokémon anticipates and dodges the attacks of its allies next to it in battle.

141.name: Moody
141.desc: At the end of every turn, one of the Pokémon’s stats will be boosted sharply, but another will be lowered. Accuracy and evasiveness are excluded.

142.name: Overcoat
142.desc: Provides immunity to weather damage from Sandstorm and Hail, and blocks all powder moves including Sleep Powder, Stun Spore, Poison Powder, Spore, Cotton Spore, Rage Powder, Powder, and Magic Powder. Also reduces incoming special damage by 20%. Multiplicative with other damage reduction sources.

143.name: Poison Touch
143.desc: Has a 30% chance to inflict poison on contact moves, both when attacking and being attacked.

144.name: Regenerator
144.desc: The Pokémon has ⅓ of its max HP restored when it switches out of battle.

145.name: Big Pecks
145.desc: Boosts the power of contact moves by 30%.

146.name: Sand Rush
146.desc: Boosts the Pokemon's Speed stat by 50% in sand. Also grants immunity to sandstorm damage.

147.name: Wonder Skin
147.desc: Immune to all damage boosting ability effects from opponents, other than Parental Bond and Multi-Headed.

148.name: Analytic
148.desc: When the user moves after the target, boosts attack power by 30%.

149.name: Illusion (N)
149.desc: The Pokémon fools opponents by entering battle disguised as the last Pokémon in its party. It reverts to its usual appearance when it takes damage from a move.

150.name: Imposter
150.desc: The Pokémon transforms into the Pokémon in front of it. It also copies all of that Pokémon’s stats apart from its HP. Copied moves have 5 PP each.

151.name: Infiltrator
151.desc: When using its moves, the Pokémon ignores the effects of targets’ Light Screen, Reflect, Aurora Veil, Safeguard, and substitutes.

152.name: Mummy
152.desc: When the Pokémon is hit by a contact move, the attacker has its Ability changed to Mummy.

153.name: Moxie
153.desc: When the Pokémon knocks out a target with an attack, it shows moxie, which boosts its Attack stat.

154.name: Justified
154.desc: Immune to Dark-type moves, and boost Attack by 1 stage when the user is hit by them.

155.name: Rattled
155.desc: The Pokémon gets scared when hit by a Dark-, Ghost-, or Bug-type attack or if intimidated, which boosts its Speed stat.

156.name: Magic Bounce
156.desc: Instead of being affected by other Pokémon’s status moves, the Pokémon bounces them back at the user.

157.name: Sap Sipper
157.desc: Immune to Grass-type moves, and boost highest attacking stat by 1 stage when the user is hit by them.

158.name: Prankster
158.desc: Status moves gain +1 priority. Status moves fail when directly targeting opposing Dark-type Pokemon.

159.name: Sand Force
159.desc: Boosts the power of the Pokémon’s Rock-, Ground-, and Steel-type moves by 30% in a sandstorm. The Pokémon takes no damage from sandstorms if it would otherwise.

160.name: Iron Barbs
160.desc: When the Pokémon is hit by a contact move, its iron barbs damages the attacker equal to ⅛ of their max HP.

161.name: Zen Mode
161.desc: The Pokémon changes its form to Zen Mode when its HP drops to ½ or less.

162.name: Victory Star
162.desc: Boosts the user's and their ally's accuracy by 1.2x when active in a double battle.

163.name: Turboblaze
163.desc: Allows moves to ignore the target's abilities and innates that interfere with effects or reduce damage. Does not bypass abilities that modify base stats such as Grass Pelt. Adds Fire to the user's typing. Retains Fire typing even upon losing the ability, going away only when switching out.

164.name: Teravolt
164.desc: Allows moves to ignore the target's abilities and innates that interfere with effects or reduce damage. Does not bypass abilities that modify base stats such as Grass Pelt. Adds Electric to the user's typing. Retains Electric typing even upon losing the ability, going away only when switching out.

165.name: Aroma Veil
165.desc: Protects the user and allies from infatuation, heal block effects, and disabling moves including Disable, Taunt, Encore, and Torment.

166.name: Flower Veil
166.desc: If the Pokémon or its allies next to it in battle are Grass-type, they are immune to status conditions and cannot have their stats lowered.

168.name: Protean
168.desc: Changes the Pokémon’s type to the type of the move it’s about to use.

169.name: Fur Coat
169.desc: Halves the damage the Pokémon takes from physical moves.

170.name: Magician
170.desc: The user steals the target's held item when landing a non-contact move if they are currently holding no item. Does not work with Mega Stones and other similar items.

171.name: Bulletproof
171.desc: The Pokémon is immune to ball and bomb moves.

172.name: Competitive
172.desc: When the Pokémon has any of its stats lowered by an opponent, its Sp. Atk stat is boosted sharply.

173.name: Strong Jaw
173.desc: Boosts the power of biting and fang moves by 30%.

174.name: Refrigerate
174.desc: Changes the user's Normal-type moves to Ice-type. If the user is Ice-type its Ice-type moves have a 10% frostbite chance, otherwise it gains Ice STAB.

175.name: Sweet Veil (P)
175.desc: The Pokémon and its allies next to it in battle cannot become drowsy or be put to sleep.

176.name: Stance Change
176.desc: The Pokémon changes into its Blade Forme when it attacks and changes into its Shield Forme when it uses the move King’s Shield.

177.name: Gale Wings
177.desc: Increases the priority of the Pokémon’s Flying-type moves by 1 stage while its HP is full.

178.name: Mega Launcher
178.desc: Boosts pulse, beam, ball, and aura moves by 30%.

179.name: Grass Pelt
179.desc: Boosts the Pokémon’s Defense stat by 50% on Grassy Terrain.

180.name: Symbiosis (N)
180.desc: When an ally consumes an item, the Pokémon gives its own held item to that ally.

181.name: Tough Claws
181.desc: Boosts the power of the Pokémon’s contact moves by 30%.

182.name: Pixilate
182.desc: Changes the user's Normal-type moves to Fairy-type. If the user is Fairy-type its Fairy-type moves have a 10% infatuate chance, otherwise it gains Fairy STAB.

183.name: Gooey
183.desc: When the Pokémon is hit by a contact move, the attacker’s Speed stat is lowered.

184.name: Aerilate
184.desc: Changes the user's Normal-type moves to Flying-type. If the user is Flying-type its Flying-type moves are 10% faster, otherwise it gains Flying STAB.

185.name: Parental Bond
185.desc: The Pokémon strikes twice, with the second strike dealing ¼ of the first attack’s power. Multistrike moves and moves that target multiple Pokémon are ignored.

186.name: Dark Aura
186.desc: Boosts the power of the Dark-type moves of all Pokémon on the field by 33%.

187.name: Fairy Aura
187.desc: Boosts the power of the Fairy-type moves of all Pokémon on the field by 33%.

188.name: Aura Break
188.desc: The effects of Fairy Aura and Dark Aura are reversed and nullified, causing affected moves to be 25% weaker.

189.name: Primordial Sea
189.desc: The Pokémon changes the weather to nullify Fire-type attacks.

190.name: Desolate Land
190.desc: The Pokémon changes the weather to nullify Water-type attacks.

191.name: Delta Stream
191.desc: The Pokémon changes the weather so that no moves are supereffective against the Flying type.

192.name: Stamina
192.desc: When hit, raises the user's Defense by 1 stage or maximizes it on critical hits. Activates on each hit of a multihit move.

193.name: Wimp Out
193.desc: The Pokémon cowardly switches out when its HP drops to ½ or less.

194.name: Emergency Exit
194.desc: The Pokémon, sensing danger, switches out when its HP drops to ½ or less.

195.name: Water Compaction
195.desc: Reduces damage from Water-type moves by 50% and raises Defense by 2 stages when hit by Water moves. Multiplicative with other sources of damage reduction. Defense boost applies after the hit lands. Activates on each hit of a multihit move.

196.name: Merciless
196.desc: Guarantees critical hits against targets who are poisoned, paralyzed, bleeding, or have their speed lowered.

197.name: Shields Down
197.desc: When its HP drops to ½ or less, the Pokémon’s shell breaks and it becomes aggressive.

198.name: Stakeout
198.desc: Doubles the damage dealt to a target that has just switched into battle.

199.name: Water Bubble
199.desc: Halves the damage the Pokémon takes from Fire-type moves and doubles the power of its Water-type moves. The Pokémon cannot be burned.

200.name: Steelworker
200.desc: Changes the user's Normal-type moves to Steel-type. If the user is Steel-type it resists Ghost and Dark, otherwise it gains Steel STAB.

201.name: Berserk
201.desc: Berserk activates when the Pokemon drops to half HP or below from an opposing attack, boosting its highest attacking stat by one stage. Includes stat stages to determine which gets boosted. Triggers only once per battle. Other damage sources that bring you to half HP or below will not activate it.

202.name: Slush Rush
202.desc: Boosts the Pokemon's Speed stat by 50% during hail. Also grants immunity to hail damage.

203.name: Long Reach
203.desc: Long Reach prevents the user from making contact with targets when using contact moves. Additionally, physical moves receive a 1.2x damage boost.

204.name: Liquid Voice
204.desc: Liquid Voice converts Normal- type sound moves to Water-type and boosts them by 20%.

205.name: Triage
205.desc: Gives priority to the Pokémon’s healing moves.

206.name: Galvanize
206.desc: Changes the user's Normal-type moves to Electric-type. If the user is Electric-type its Electric-type moves have a 10% chance to paralyze, otherwise it gains Electric STAB.

207.name: Surge Surfer
207.desc: Surge Surfer boosts the Pokemon's Speed by 50% when Electric Terrain is active. The boost applies immediately and disappears when it ends.

208.name: Schooling
208.desc: The Pokémon forms a powerful school when it reaches level 20. It stops schooling when its HP drops to ¼ or less.

209.name: Disguise
209.desc: Once per battle, the shroud that covers the Pokémon can protect it from an attack at the cost of losing ⅛ of its max HP.

210.name: Battle Bond
210.desc: Once per switch-in when the Pokémon knocks out a target, its Attack, Sp. Atk, and Speed stats are boosted. If a Greninja has this ability, it instead transforms into Ash-Greninja whenever it knocks out a target.

211.name: Power Construct
211.desc: Cells gather to aid the Pokémon when its HP drops to ½ or less, causing it to change into its Complete Forme.

212.name: Corrosion
212.desc: The Pokémon can poison or badly poison targets even if they’re Steel or Poison types.

213.name: Comatose
213.desc: The Pokémon is always drowsing and can attack while in its sleeping state. It is considered under the effects of a status condition.

214.name: Queenly Majesty
214.desc: The Pokémon’s majesty pressures opponents and makes them unable to use priority moves against the Pokémon or its allies next to it in battle.

215.name: Innards Out
215.desc: When the Pokémon takes damage from a move that knocks it out, it deals the same amount of damage to the attacker.

216.name: Dancer
216.desc: Whenever a dance move is used in battle, the Pokémon will copy the user to immediately perform that dance move itself.

217.name: Battery
217.desc: Allies next to the Pokémon in battle have their Sp. Atk increased by 30% when using special moves.

218.name: Fluffy
218.desc: Halves the damage taken from moves that make direct contact, but doubles that of Fire-type moves.

219.name: Dazzling
219.desc: The Pokémon dazzles its opponents, making them unable to use priority moves against the Pokémon or its allies next to it in battle.

220.name: Soul-Heart
220.desc: Boosts the Pokémon’s Sp. Atk stat every time another Pokémon faints.

221.name: Tangling Hair
221.desc: When the Pokémon is hit by a contact move, the attacker’s Speed stat is lowered.

222.name: Receiver
222.desc: The Pokémon changes its Ability to match that of a defeated ally in a Double Battle.

223.name: Power of Alchemy
223.desc: Upon entry, transmutes all opposing Berries into Black Sludge. When any Pokemon loses an item during battle, it gets replaced by Black Sludge. If Black Sludge is removed, it gets replaced by Big Nugget.

224.name: Beast Boost
224.desc: Boosts the Pokémon’s most proficient stat every time it knocks out a target.

225.name: RKS System
225.desc: RKS System changes the user's form based on its held Memory disc. Before each attack, the user overrides their type to match the move's type. Boosts STAB from 1.5x to 2.0x damage.

226.name: Electric Surge
226.desc: Turns the ground into Electric Terrain for 5 turns when the Pokémon enters a battle.

227.name: Psychic Surge
227.desc: Creates Psychic Terrain for 8 turns (12 with Terrain Extender) on entry. Grounded Pokemon are immune to priority moves from opponents. Psychic moves gain 30% power boost. Expanding Force hits all foes with increased power. Nature Power becomes Psychic. Overrides other existing terrains.

228.name: Misty Surge
228.desc: Creates Misty Terrain on entry, lasting 8 turns (12 with Terrain Extender). Misty Terrain prevents all status conditions for grounded Pokemon and boosts Fairy-type moves by 30%. Overrides existing terrain.

229.name: Grassy Surge
229.desc: Creates Grassy Terrain for 8 turns (12 with Terrain Extender) on entry. Grounded Pokemon heal 1/16 HP per turn and Grass moves gain 30% power. Overrides other terrains.

230.name: Full Metal Body
230.desc: The Pokémon’s stats cannot be lowered by other Pokémon’s moves or Abilities. This effect cannot be ignored.

231.name: Shadow Shield
231.desc: Halves the damage the Pokémon takes while its HP is full. This effect cannot be ignored by other Pokémon’s moves or Abilities.

232.name: Prism Armor
232.desc: Reduces damage from super- effective attacks by 35%. Multiplicative with other sources of damage reduction.

233.name: Neuroforce
233.desc: Super effective attacks are boosted by 35%.

234.name: Intrepid Sword
234.desc: Boosts the Pokémon’s Attack stat when the Pokémon enters a battle.

235.name: Dauntless Shield
235.desc: Boosts the Pokémon’s Defense stat when the Pokémon enters a battle.

236.name: Libero
236.desc: Changes the Pokémon’s type to the type of the move it’s about to use.

237.name: Ball Fetch
237.desc: The Pokémon will fetch the Poké Ball from the first failed throw of the battle, even if it did not participate.

238.name: Cotton Down
238.desc: When the Pokémon is hit by an attack, it scatters cotton fluff around and lowers the Speed stat of all Pokémon except itself.

239.name: Propeller Tail
239.desc: Propeller Tail boosts the user's Speed by 50% during rain and grants immunity to redirection effects. The speed boost works in all forms of rain.

240.name: Mirror Armor
240.desc: Instead of being affected by stat-lowering effects, the Pokémon bounces them back at whichever Pokémon caused them.

241.name: Gulp Missile
241.desc: When the Pokémon uses Surf or Dive, it catches prey in its mouth. Taking damage causes the Pokémon to spit its prey at the attacker, dealing ¼ of the their max HP in damage. Depending on the caught prey, it will also lower the opponent’s Defense stat or inflict paralysis.

242.name: Stalwart
242.desc: Can't have its moves redirected, can't be critically hit, and can't have its abilities suppressed. Ability suppression includes effects such as Mold Breaker, Gastro Acid, Neutralizing Gas, and Mycelium Might.

243.name: Steam Engine
243.desc: Boosts the Speed stat by 6 stages when the Pokémon is hit by a Fire- or Water-type move.

244.name: Punk Rock
244.desc: Boosts the power of the Pokémon’s sound-based moves by 30%. The Pokémon also takes half the damage from these kinds of moves

245.name: Sand Spit
245.desc: Summons a sandstorm for 5 turns when the Pokémon takes damage from moves.

246.name: Ice Scales
246.desc: The Pokémon is protected by ice scales, which halve the damage taken from special moves.

247.name: Ripen
247.desc: Ripens and doubles the effect of berries eaten by the Pokémon.

248.name: Ice Face
248.desc: The Pokémon’s ice head can take a physical attack as a substitute, the ice will be restored when it begins to snow or hail.

249.name: Power Spot
249.desc: Allies next to the Pokémon in battle deal 30% increased damage.

250.name: Mimicry
250.desc: The Pokémon’s type changes depending on the terrain.

251.name: Screen Cleaner
251.desc: When the Pokémon enters a battle, the effects of Light Screen, Reflect, and Aurora Veil are nullified for both opposing and ally Pokémon.

252.name: Steely Spirit
252.desc: Steely Spirit increases the power of Steel-type moves by 30% for both the user and its allies in battle.

253.name: Perish Body
253.desc: When the Pokémon is hit by a contact move, the Pokémon and the attacker will faint after three turns unless they switch out of battle.

254.name: Wandering Spirit
254.desc: When the Pokémon is hit by a contact move, it swaps Abilities with the attacker.

255.name: Gorilla Tactics
255.desc: Boosts the Pokémon’s Attack stat by 50%, but only allows the use of the first selected move.

256.name: Neutralizing Gas
256.desc: While the Pokémon is in the battle, the effects of all other Pokémon’s Abilities will be nullified or will not be triggered.

257.name: Pastel Veil
257.desc: Pastel Veil automatically sets up Safeguard for the user's team when the Pokemon enters battle. Safeguard lasts for 5 turns and protects all team members from status conditions.

258.name: Hunger Switch
258.desc: The Pokémon changes its form, alternating between its Full Belly Mode and Hangry Mode after the end of every turn.

259.name: Quick Draw
259.desc: The Pokémon’s moves have a 30% chance of going first among moves of the same priority

260.name: Unseen Fist
260.desc: When the Pokémon uses contact moves, it can hit even targets that are protecting themselves, dealing ¼ of the damage that the move would otherwise deal. Everything aside from the target’s protective effects is still triggered.

261.name: Curious Medicine
261.desc: When the Pokémon enters a battle, it scatters medicine from its shell, which removes all stat changes from allies.

262.name: Transistor
262.desc: Transistor increases the power of all Electric-type moves by 50%. Stacks additively with other damage modifiers.

263.name: Dragon’s Maw
263.desc: Boosts the power of the Pokémon’s Dragon-type moves by 50%.

264.name: Chilling Neigh
264.desc: When the Pokémon knocks out a target, it utters a chilling neigh, which boosts its Attack stat.

265.name: Grim Neigh
265.desc: When the Pokémon knocks out a target, it utters a terrifying neigh, which boosts its Sp. Atk stat.

266.name: As One
266.desc: This Ability combines the effects of both Unnerve and Chilling Neigh, preventing opposing Pokémon from consuming berries and boosting the Pokémon’s Attack stat when knocking out a target.

267.name: As One
267.desc: This Ability combines the effects of both Unnerve and Grim Neigh, preventing opposing Pokémon from consuming berries and boosting the Pokémon’s Sp. Atk stat when knocking out a target.

268.name: Lingering Aroma
268.desc: When the Pokémon is hit by a contact move, the attacker has its Ability changed to Lingering Aroma.

269.name: Seed Sower
269.desc: Turns the ground into Grassy Terrain for 5 turns when the Pokémon takes damage from moves.

270.name: Thermal Exchange
270.desc: Boosts the Attack stat when the Pokémon is hit by a Fire-type move. The Pokémon also cannot be burned.

271.name: Anger Shell
271.desc: When dropping to below 50% HP, the user triggers Shell Smash effects: raising Attack, Special Attack, and Speed by 2 stages each while lowering Defense and Special Defense by 1 stage each. Only activates once per battle. Activates at the last hit of multihit moves.

272.name: Purifying Salt
272.desc: The Pokémon’s pure salt protects it from status conditions and halves the damage taken from Ghost-type moves.

273.name: Well-Baked Body
273.desc: The Pokémon takes no damage when hit by Fire-type moves. Instead, its Defense stat is sharply boosted.

274.name: Wind Rider
274.desc: Boosts the Pokémon’s Attack stat if Tailwind takes effect or if the Pokémon is hit by a wind move. The Pokémon also takes no damage from wind moves.

275.name: Guard Dog
275.desc: Boosts the Pokémon’s Attack stat if intimidated. Moves and items that would force the Pokémon to switch out also fail to work.

276.name: Rocky Payload
276.desc: Boosts the power of the Pokémon’s Rock-type moves by 50%.

277.name: Wind Power
277.desc: The Pokémon becomes charged when Tailwind takes effect or it is hit by a wind move, boosting the power of the next Electric-type move the Pokémon uses.

278.name: Zero to Hero
278.desc: The Pokémon transforms into its Hero Form when it switches out of battle.

279.name: Commander
279.desc: Upon entering battle, the Pokémon goes inside an ally Dondozo if one is on the field and raises all its stats sharply. If this Pokémon is your lead into battle, it raises the likelihood of double battles.

280.name: Electromorphosis
280.desc: When the Pokémon takes damage from a move, the power of the next Electric-type move used by the Pokémon will be doubled.

281.name: Protosynthesis
281.desc: Boosts the Pokémon’s most proficient stat in harsh sunlight by 30%, or 50% if the stat boosted is its Speed stat.

282.name: Quark Drive
282.desc: Boosts the Pokémon’s most proficient stat on Electric Terrain by 30%, or 50% if the stat boosted is its Speed stat.

283.name: Good as Gold
283.desc: A body of pure, solid gold gives the Pokémon full immunity to other Pokémon’s status moves.

284.name: Vessel of Ruin
284.desc: The power of the Pokémon’s ruinous vessel lowers the Sp. Atk stats of all Pokémon except itself by 25% while on the field.

285.name: Sword of Ruin
285.desc: The power of the Pokémon’s ruinous sword lowers the Defense stats of all Pokémon except itself by 25% while on the field.

286.name: Tablets of Ruin
286.desc: The power of the Pokémon’s ruinous wooden tablets lowers the Attack stats of all Pokémon except itself by 25% while on the field.

287.name: Beads of Ruin
287.desc: The power of the Pokémon’s ruinous beads lowers the Sp. Def stats of all Pokémon except itself by 25% while on the field.

288.name: Orichalcum Pulse
288.desc: Summons harsh sunlight for 5 turns when the Pokémon enters a battle. The ancient pulse thrumming through the Pokémon also boosts its Attack stat by 33% in harsh sunlight.

289.name: Hadron Engine
289.desc: Turns the ground into Electric Terrain for 5 turns when the Pokémon enters a battle. The futuristic engine within the Pokémon also boosts its Sp. Atk stat by 33% on Electric Terrain.

290.name: Opportunist
290.desc: Grants +1 priority to all moves when targeting opponents with 50% HP or less.

291.name: Cud Chew
291.desc: If the Pokémon eats a Berry, it will regurgitate and eat that same Berry once more at the end of next turn.

292.name: Sharpness
292.desc: Boosts the power of the Pokémon’s slicing moves by 50%.

293.name: Supreme Overlord
293.desc: When the Pokémon enters a battle, the power of its moves is boosted by 10% for each Pokémon in its party that has been defeated in battle already. The maximum boost is 50%

294.name: Costar
294.desc: When the Pokémon enters a double battle, it copies its ally’s stat changes.

295.name: Toxic Debris
295.desc: Scatters poison spikes at the feet of the opposing team when the Pokémon takes damage from physical moves.

296.name: Armor Tail
296.desc: The mysterious tail covering the Pokémon’s head makes opponents unable to use priority moves against the Pokémon or its allies next to it in battle.

297.name: Earth Eater
297.desc: Ground-type moves do not work on the Pokémon. Instead, they restore ¼ of its max HP.

298.name: Mycelium Might
298.desc: The Pokémon’s status moves will go last among moves of the same priority, but these moves will be unaffected by the Ability of the target.

299.name: Mind’s Eye
299.desc: The Pokémon ignores changes to opponents’ evasiveness, its accuracy can’t be lowered, and it can hit Ghost types with Normal- and Fighting-type moves.

300.name: Supersweet Syrup
300.desc: The user's item cannot be forcibly removed or stolen. When making contact, the opponent's item is disabled for 2 turns.

301.name: Hospitality
301.desc: When the Pokémon enters a battle, it showers its ally with hospitality, restoring ¼ of its ally’s max HP.

302.name: Toxic Chain
302.desc: When the Pokémon hits a target with a move, the target has a 30% chance of being badly poisoned from the power of the Pokémon’s toxic chain.

303.name: Embody Aspect
303.desc: The Pokémon’s heart fills with memories, causing the Teal Mask to shine and the Pokémon’s Speed stat to be boosted.

304.name: Embody Aspect
304.desc: The Pokémon’s heart fills with memories, causing the Wellspring Mask to shine and the Pokémon’s Sp. Def stat to be boosted.

305.name: Embody Aspect
305.desc: The Pokémon’s heart fills with memories, causing the Hearthflame Mask to shine and the Pokémon’s Attack stat to be boosted.

306.name: Embody Aspect
306.desc: The Pokémon’s heart fills with memories, causing the Cornerstone Mask to shine and the Pokémon’s Defense stat to be boosted.

307.name: Tera Shift
307.desc: When the Pokémon enters a battle, it absorbs the energy around itself and transforms into its Terastal Form.

308.name: Tera Shell
308.desc: The Pokémon’s shell contains the powers of each type. All damage-dealing moves that hit the Pokémon when its HP is full will not be very effective.

309.name: Teraform Zero
309.desc: At full HP, all attacks towards the user deal not very effective damage regardless of type effectiveness. Activates on each hit of a multihit attack unlike other similar abilities. On entry, clears normal weathers and terrains.

310.name: Poison Puppeteer
310.desc: Pokémon poisoned or badly poisoned by this Pokémon’s moves will also become confused.
